answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'fish' is made of three individual sounds: f, i, and sh. Option B is correct because the word 'shop' is made of three individual sounds: sh, o, and p. Option D is correct because the word 'sun' is made of three individual sounds: s, short u, and n. Option C is incorrect because the word 'spot' has four individual sounds (s, p, o, t), not three; it keeps the blend 'sp' together instead of separating it into individual sounds.
subject: ELA

question: Which words have the /sh/ sound made by the letters 's' and 'h' working together? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The words 'ship', 'fish', and 'shop' each contain the consonant digraph 'sh', where the two letters together represent the single sound /sh/. In 'ship' and 'shop', this digraph appears at the beginning of the word, while in 'fish' it appears at the end. The word 'sip' is incorrect because it contains only the single letter 's' making the /s/ sound, not the digraph 'sh'.
subject: ELA

question: Read each word. Which words have the two letters 's' and 'h' working together to make the /sh/ sound? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'washing' contains the consonant digraph 'sh' in the middle of the word (wash-ing), where the letters 's' and 'h' work together to make a single /sh/ sound. Option B is correct because the word 'brush' ends with the consonant digraph 'sh,' where the two letters combine to make the /sh/ sound. Option C is incorrect because although 'chip' has two letters at the beginning that work together, it contains the digraph 'ch' which makes the /ch/ sound, not the /sh/ sound. Option D is incorrect because 'think' begins with the consonant digraph 'th,' which makes the /th/ sound, not the /sh/ sound.
subject: ELA

question: Which words contain a consonant digraph (two letters that work together to make one sound)? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'fish' ends with the consonant digraph 'sh,' where the letters 's' and 'h' work together to make the single /sh/ sound. Option B is correct because the word 'chip' begins with the consonant digraph 'ch,' where the letters 'c' and 'h' work together to make the single /ch/ sound. Option D is correct because the word 'bath' ends with the consonant digraph 'th,' where the letters 't' and 'h' work together to make the single /th/ sound. Option C is not correct because the word 'lamp' does not contain a consonant digraph; each consonant letter 'l,' 'm,' and 'p' represents its own individual sound.
subject: ELA

question: Read each word below. Select ALL the words that are real one-syllable words you can read. (Select all that apply)
answer_explanation: 'Sad' (A) and 'hen' (B) are real one-syllable words with regular CVC spellings that students can decode. 'Fep' (C) and 'vam' (D) are nonsense words that follow regular spelling patterns but are not real English words, so they should not be selected.
subject: ELA

question: Read each word below. Select ALL the words that are real, one-syllable words spelled correctly.
answer_explanation: Option A (jump), Option C (lump), and Option D (limp) are all real, regularly spelled one-syllable words that follow the CVCC pattern with a final consonant blend (/mp/). 'Jump' uses the short vowel sound /ŭ/ represented by the letter 'u' between the initial consonant /j/ and the final blend /mp/. 'Lump' uses the short vowel sound /ŭ/ represented by the letter 'u' with the initial consonant /l/. 'Limp' uses the short vowel sound /ĭ/ represented by the letter 'i' with the initial consonant /l/. These words can be decoded using regular phonics rules for one-syllable words.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following are real words? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A (jump) is correct because it is a regularly spelled one-syllable CVCC word with a final consonant blend (/mp/), mapping sounds /j/, /ŭ/, /m/, /p/. Option C (lump) is correct because it is also a regularly spelled one-syllable CVCC word with a final consonant blend (/mp/), mapping sounds /l/, /ŭ/, /m/, /p/. Option B (jamp) is incorrect because although it follows a similar letter pattern, it is not a real English word. Option D (jomp) is incorrect because it is not a real English word; the vowel sound and letter combination do not form a valid word.
subject: ELA

question: Which words use a final –e or a vowel team to make a long vowel sound? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'cake' uses a final –e to make the vowel 'a' say its name (long a sound). Option B is correct because 'rain' uses the vowel team 'ai' to make the vowel 'a' say its name (long a sound). Option C is incorrect because 'cat' has a short vowel sound and does not use a final –e or vowel team. Option D is incorrect because 'pen' has a short vowel sound and does not use a final –e or vowel team.
subject: ELA

question: Look at each word below. Which words use a vowel team or a final silent e to spell a long vowel sound? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'rain' uses the vowel team 'ai' to spell the long 'a' sound. Option B is correct because 'Pete' uses the final silent 'e' to make the first 'e' say its name, the long 'e' sound. Option C is correct because 'boat' uses the vowel team 'oa' to spell the long 'o' sound. Option D is incorrect because 'head' contains the vowel team 'ea', but in this word it spells the short 'e' sound rather than a long vowel sound.
subject: ELA

question: Look at each word below. Which words have a long vowel sound made by a final -e or a vowel team? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'made' has a final silent -e that makes the 'a' say its name, creating a long vowel sound. The word 'sleep' has the vowel team 'ee' that works together to make a long 'e' sound.
subject: ELA

question: Look at these words. Which words have two syllables? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'sister' has two syllables: sis-ter. Each syllable must have a vowel sound. The first syllable 'sis' has the vowel sound 'i', and the second syllable 'ter' has the vowel sound 'e'. Option C is correct because 'napkin' has two syllables: nap-kin. The first syllable 'nap' has the vowel sound 'a', and the second syllable 'kin' has the vowel sound 'i'. Option B is incorrect because 'cup' has only one vowel sound (the 'u'), so it has only one syllable. Option D is incorrect because 'banana' has three vowel sounds (the three 'a's), so it has three syllables: ba-nan-a.
subject: ELA

question: Look at each word. Which words have exactly four syllables? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: To find the number of syllables, listen for the vowel sounds in each word. Every syllable has one vowel sound. Option A is correct because 'caterpillar' has four vowel sounds and four syllables: cat-er-pil-lar. Option B is incorrect because 'elephant' has three vowel sounds and three syllables: el-e-phant. Option C is correct because 'alligator' has four vowel sounds and four syllables: al-li-ga-tor. Option D is correct because 'watermelon' has four vowel sounds and four syllables: wa-ter-mel-on.
subject: ELA

question: The word 'basket' has two syllables: bas-ket. Which other words have exactly two syllables? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'basket' has two syllables because it contains two vowel sounds: the short 'a' sound in 'bas' and the short 'e' sound in 'ket.' Every syllable must have one vowel sound. Option A is correct because 'pencil' has two syllables (pen-cil): the first syllable 'pen' has the short 'e' sound and the second syllable 'cil' has the short 'i' sound. Option C is correct because 'robot' has two syllables (ro-bot): the first syllable 'ro' has the long 'o' sound and the second syllable 'bot' has the short 'o' sound. Option B is incorrect because 'cat' has only one vowel sound (the short 'a'), so it has only one syllable. Option D is incorrect because 'butterfly' has three syllables (but-ter-fly) with three vowel sounds (short 'u', 'er' sound, and long 'i'), so it does not match the two-syllable count of 'basket.'
subject: ELA

question: Look at how each word is broken into parts. Which words are split into syllables the right way? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'cupcake' is a two-syllable compound word that is correctly split as cup-cake. Option B is correct because 'napkin' is a two-syllable word following a CVC-CVC pattern that is correctly split as nap-kin. Option C is correct because 'basket' is a two-syllable word that is correctly split as bas-ket. Option D does not apply here because 'dog' is a one-syllable word and should not be split into two parts.
subject: ELA

question: Look at how each word is broken into parts. Which words are split into syllables correctly? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A (kit-ten) is correct because 'kitten' follows the VCCV pattern with double consonants 'tt' in the middle; we divide between them to create two closed syllables (kit and ten). Option B (cup-cake) is correct because 'cupcake' is a compound word made of two smaller words, 'cup' and 'cake', so we split at the word boundary; the first syllable is closed (CVC) and the second follows the VCe pattern. Option C (but-ton) is correct because 'button' has double consonants 'tt', so we split between them to form two closed syllables (but and ton). Option D (ba-sket) is incorrect because 'basket' has two consonants 'sk' between the vowels; following the VCCV pattern, we must split between the two consonants to form 'bas-ket', not 'ba-sket'.
subject: ELA

question: Look at how each word is split into parts. Which words are split into syllables correctly? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'cupcake' is a two-syllable compound word split as cup-cake. 'Cup' is a closed syllable (CVC), and 'cake' follows a VCe pattern with a silent e. Option B is correct because 'kitten' is split as kit-ten between the double consonants 'tt', creating two closed syllables. Option D is correct because 'rabbit' is split as rab-bit between the double consonants 'bb', creating two closed syllables. Option C is incorrect because 'napkin' should be split as nap-kin (after the 'p'), not na-pkin; 'nap' is a closed syllable with the short vowel sound, and splitting after 'na' would separate the consonant from its vowel.
subject: ELA

question: Read the words. Which words have the inflectional ending -ing? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A ('jumping') and option C ('walking') are correct because they both end with -ing, which is an inflectional ending added to the base words 'jump' and 'walk' to show the action is happening now. Option B ('cats') ends with -s, which is a different inflectional ending used to mean more than one. Option D ('play') is incorrect because it is the base word with no inflectional ending added.
subject: ELA

question: Which words have an inflectional ending? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Washing (Option A) has the inflectional ending -ing added to the base word 'wash,' showing an action happening now. Wishes (Option B) has the inflectional ending -es added to 'wish,' showing more than one. Passed (Option D) has the inflectional ending -ed added to 'pass,' showing the action happened in the past. Wing (Option C) does not have an inflectional ending; it is a base word where 'ing' is part of the root, not a suffix added to change the word's meaning.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence.

The cats played with the ball and are sleeping now.

Which words have an inflectional ending like -s, -ed, or -ing? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'cats' is made of the base word 'cat' plus the inflectional ending '-s.' The word 'played' is made of the base word 'play' plus the inflectional ending '-ed.' The word 'sleeping' is made of the base word 'sleep' plus the inflectional ending '-ing.' The word 'ball' is a base word and does not have an inflectional ending added to it.
subject: ELA

question: Look at the words below. Which words are irregularly spelled and do not follow the usual letter sounds? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'was' is irregularly spelled because the letter 'a' represents the /ʌ/ (short u) or schwa sound, and the 's' is pronounced /z/, instead of the expected short 'a' /æ/ and /s/ sounds. The word 'said' is irregularly spelled because the letters 'ai' represent the /ɛ/ (short e) sound instead of the long 'a' /eɪ/ sound. The words 'cat' and 'sun' are regularly spelled because they follow the consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) pattern where each letter makes its expected sound.
subject: ELA

question: Read these words. Which words are irregularly spelled because the silent 'e' at the end does NOT make the vowel say its name? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'have' (Option A) is irregularly spelled because the 'a' represents the short /æ/ sound (as in 'cat'), even though the silent 'e' at the end normally makes 'a' say its name (/eɪ/). The word 'give' (Option C) is irregularly spelled because the 'i' represents the short /ɪ/ sound (as in 'sit'), even though the silent 'e' at the end normally makes 'i' say its name (/aɪ/). The word 'like' (Option B) is correctly spelled following regular phonics rules: the silent 'e' makes the 'i' say its name. The word 'made' (Option D) is also correctly spelled following regular phonics rules: the silent 'e' makes the 'a' say its name.
subject: ELA

question: Look at these words. Which ones are sight words that do not follow the usual spelling rules? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'said' is an irregularly spelled word because the letters 'ai' make the short e sound (like in 'bed') instead of the usual long a sound (like in 'rain'). The word 'was' is irregular because the letter 'a' makes the short u sound and the 's' makes the z sound, which do not follow the usual rules for those letters. The word 'cat' is not correct because it is a regularly spelled word where each letter makes its most common sound. The word 'sun' is not correct because it is also a regularly spelled word where each letter makes its most common sound.
subject: ELA

question: Read these sentences. We see the moon at night. The moon looks bright. Which sentences tell about the moon? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: We see the moon at night.
answer_options.C: We see the moon at day.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the first sentence states that we see the moon at night. Option B is correct because the second sentence states that the moon looks bright.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text: "Ben finds a smooth stone by the river. It is dark gray and cold. He puts it in his pocket to show his dad." Which sentences are true based on the text? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Ben finds a stone near water.
answer_options.C: Ben puts the stone in his bag.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states Ben finds the stone 'by the river,' which means near water. Option B is correct because the text says the stone 'is dark gray and cold,' meaning it feels cold. Option C is incorrect because the text specifies Ben puts the stone in his 'pocket,' not a bag. Option D is incorrect because the text describes the stone as 'dark gray,' not light gray.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. Kim gets a new book. She opens it to. The book has many pages. Which sentences tell what Kim does in the story? Select ALL that apply?
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that Kim gets a new book. Option B is correct because the text says she opens it to look at the pictures, which means she looks at them. Option D is correct because the text explicitly states that she opens the book. Option C is incorrect because the text says Kim opens the book, not closes it.
subject: ELA

question: Mia is practicing reading her book out loud. Which choices show she is reading with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Read at a speed that is not too fast and not too slow
answer_options.B: Use a sad voice when the story says the puppy is lost
answer_options.C: Skip the hard words to finish quickly
answer_options.D: Look at each word carefully and say it correctly

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because reading at a just-right speed shows appropriate rate. Option B is correct because changing her voice to match the feelings in the story shows expression. Option D is correct because looking at each word and saying it correctly shows accuracy. Option C is incorrect because skipping words means not reading with accuracy, which is not fluent reading.
subject: ELA

question: Eli is reading a short story aloud for the second time to practice his fluency. Which actions demonstrate that he is reading with accuracy, appropriate rate, or expression? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: He self-corrects when he reads 'ship' as 'sip' so the sentence makes sense.
answer_options.B: He reads as fast as possible even if he skips some words.
answer_options.C: He makes his voice sound excited when the character wins a prize.
answer_options.D: He rereads a tricky sentence smoothly after practicing it once.

answer_explanation: Option A demonstrates accuracy because Eli notices his mistake and fixes it to ensure the text makes sense. Option C demonstrates expression because he adjusts his voice to match the character's emotions. Option D demonstrates appropriate rate and fluency on successive readings because he practices the sentence again to read it smoothly. Option B is incorrect because reading too fast and skipping words sacrifices accuracy, which is not fluent reading.
subject: ELA

question: Sofia is reading a story aloud to her teacher. Which actions show that Sofia is reading with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: She reads each word correctly without skipping any words.
answer_options.B: She reads at a smooth pace that is not too fast or too slow.
answer_options.C: She changes her voice to show how the characters are feeling.
answer_options.D: She reads as fast as she can, even if she misses some words.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because reading with accuracy means saying every word correctly without skipping or guessing. Option B is correct because appropriate rate means reading at a smooth, steady pace that sounds natural and is easy for listeners to understand. Option C is correct because expression means using your voice to show emotions, punctuation, and character feelings. Option D is incorrect because reading as fast as possible while missing words shows a lack of accuracy and an inappropriate rate; fluent reading requires focusing on correctness and pacing rather than speed alone.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentence: 'We saw the s___ at the zoo.' Which words could fit in the blank to make the sentence make sense? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a seal is an animal that lives at the zoo. Option B is correct because a snake is an animal you can see at the zoo. Option C is correct because a sheep can be found at a zoo or petting zoo. Option D is incorrect because soap is not an animal and is not found at the zoo.
subject: ELA

question: Tom read the sentence: 'Wipe your shoes on the r_g.' At first, he read the last word as 'rag,' but that did not make sense. Which sentences would help Tom use context to figure out the right word? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It is a small carpet by the door.
answer_options.B: It lies flat on the floor.
answer_options.C: You use it to wash the car.
answer_options.D: It is made from old towels.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because describing the object as a 'small carpet by the door' matches the meaning of 'rug' and fits the context of wiping shoes. Option B is correct because stating that it 'lies flat on the floor' provides context that confirms the object is a rug placed at the door. Option C is incorrect because washing a car is an action associated with a 'rag,' not a 'rug,' so this clue would not help Tom self-correct to the right word. Option D is incorrect because being made from old towels describes a 'rag,' which would reinforce Tom's initial mistake rather than help him fix it.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentences.

It is a hot summer day. The kids want to cool off. They jump in the _____.

Which words make sense in the blank? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A (pool) and Option B (lake) are correct because the context describes a hot summer day where the kids want to cool off; pools and lakes are places with water where people go to cool down. Option C (snow) is incorrect because snow is found in winter, not hot summer days, and jumping in snow would not help cool off during summer. Option D (oven) is incorrect because ovens are hot appliances used for cooking, not places to jump in to cool off.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text about birds. Birds build nests in trees. They sing in the morning. They have feathers. Which sentences tell about birds? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that 'Birds build nests in trees.' Option B is correct because the text says 'They sing in the morning.' Option C is correct because the text mentions 'They have feathers.' Option D is incorrect because the text does not say that birds swim in the ocean; this detail is not found in the passage.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following demonstrate the skill described in RI.1.1 at hard difficulty? (Select ALL that apply)

answer_options.A: A correct choice that matches the standard.
answer_options.B: Another correct choice that also matches.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they match the skill described in the standard. Options C and D are incorrect.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text about squirrels.

Squirrels gather food in the fall. They bury nuts under trees. At night, squirrels sleep in their nests.

Which sentences from the text tell WHEN the squirrels do something? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Squirrels gather food in the fall.
answer_options.C: At night, squirrels sleep in their nests.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the phrase 'in the fall' tells when the squirrels gather food. Option C is correct because 'At night' tells when the squirrels sleep. Option B is incorrect because it tells where the squirrels bury nuts (under trees), not when they do it. Option D is incorrect because the text does not say that squirrels play in the spring.
subject: ELA

question: Which informational books are good for a first grader to read with a teacher's help? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A book about dogs that has photos and short sentences.
answer_options.B: A book about space that has hard words and no pictures.
answer_options.C: A book about plants that has labels on the pictures.
answer_options.D: A book about history that has long chapters and small print.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because photographs provide visual support and short sentences make the text appropriately complex for grade 1 readers with prompting. Option C is correct because picture labels are supportive text features that help first graders understand new vocabulary, making the text appropriately complex.
subject: ELA

question: Which features show that an informational book is appropriately complex for a first grader to read with a teacher's help? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It has some new science words, but there are pictures that explain what they mean.
answer_options.B: The sentences are short and tell one idea at a time.
answer_options.C: It has a table of contents to help find the topics.
answer_options.D: It has long paragraphs on every page with no pictures to help.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because informational texts for first graders can include domain-specific vocabulary when supported by pictures or glossaries to aid understanding. Option B is correct because appropriately complex texts for this grade level use short sentences that express single ideas to support emerging reading skills. Option C is correct because text features like a table of contents provide scaffolding that helps first graders navigate informational texts with teacher support. Option D is incorrect because long paragraphs without picture support represent text complexity that is too difficult for first grade readers, even with support.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these sentences from a book about cats.

Cats are pets. They have soft fur and whiskers. Cats like to chase toys. They drink milk or water.

Which sentences tell facts from the book? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: Cats have soft fur and whiskers.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the first sentence states that cats are pets. Option B is correct because the second sentence says they have soft fur and whiskers. Option C is incorrect because the book does not say cats can talk like people. Option D is incorrect because the book does not say cats live in trees; it says they are pets.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text. "Frogs can be green. They can jump high. Frogs like to sit on lily pads." Which sentences tell key details from the text? Select all that apply.

answer_options.C: Frogs like to sit on lily pads.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that frogs can be green. Option B is correct because the text says they can jump high. Option C is correct because the text mentions frogs like to sit on lily pads. Option D is incorrect because the text does not say that frogs sleep all winter; it only talks about frogs being green, jumping high, and sitting on lily pads.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text. 'Frogs are animals that go through big changes. They start as tiny eggs in the water. The eggs hatch into tadpoles. Tadpoles have long tails to help them swim. When they grow up, they lose their tails and grow legs to become adult frogs.' Which choices tell key details about how frogs grow and change? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Frogs begin as eggs in the water.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that frogs start as tiny eggs in the water, which is a key detail about the beginning of a frog's life cycle. Option B is correct because the text explains that tadpoles have long tails to help them swim, which is a key detail about the tadpole stage.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. 'Cats are pets. They have soft fur. Cats like to sleep.' Which sentences tell key details from the text? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the first sentence of the text states that cats are pets. Option B is correct because the second sentence states that cats have soft fur. Option D is correct because the third sentence states that cats like to sleep. Option C is incorrect because the text does not say that cats are big.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentences.

The farmer put the pots near the window so they could get sunlight. The plants grew big and strong.

Which sentences describe the connection between what the farmer did and how the plants grew? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The farmer moved the pots to the window.
answer_options.B: The plants got sunlight from the window.
answer_options.C: The plants grew big because they had light.
answer_options.D: The farmer watered the plants every day.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it describes the farmer's action that started the connection. Option B is correct because it explains how the plants got what they needed from the farmer's action. Option C is correct because it describes the result of the connection between the sunlight and the plant growth. Option D is not supported by the text because the sentences do not mention the farmer watering the plants.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text about bees and flowers.

"Bees fly to flowers to drink sweet nectar. Pollen sticks to their fuzzy bodies. Bees carry the pollen to other flowers. The flowers need the pollen to grow seeds."

How are the bees and the flowers connected? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Bees give nectar to the flowers.
answer_options.B: Bees carry pollen that helps flowers grow seeds.
answer_options.C: Flowers give bees food to eat.
answer_options.D: Bees use flowers to build their homes.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text states that bees carry pollen to other flowers and that flowers need this pollen to grow seeds, showing a connection where bees help flowers reproduce. Option C is correct because the text says bees fly to flowers to drink sweet nectar, which means the flowers provide food for the bees. Option A is incorrect because the text states bees take nectar from flowers to drink, not that they give nectar to flowers. Option D is incorrect because the text does not mention bees using flowers to build homes; it only describes bees visiting flowers for food and pollen.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences about Sam and Sara. "Sam is a baker. He bakes bread every morning. Sara owns a small shop. She sells Sam's bread to people in the town." What is the connection between Sam and Sara? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Sam bakes the bread that Sara sells.
answer_options.B: Sara helps Sam by selling his bread.
answer_options.C: Sam buys bread from Sara's shop.
answer_options.D: Sara teaches Sam how to bake.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that Sam bakes bread and Sara sells it, showing the connection between the two people. Option B is correct because Sara selling Sam's bread helps him reach customers who want to buy it. Option C is incorrect because the text states that Sara sells Sam's bread, not that Sam buys bread from Sara. Option D is incorrect because the text does not say that Sara teaches Sam; it only says she sells his bread.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences: 'The pond was frozen. The water was hard and icy.' Which words help you understand what 'frozen' means? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'hard' describes how frozen water feels, which helps clarify the meaning of 'frozen.' Option B is correct because 'icy' tells us the water is like ice, which also helps explain what 'frozen' means. Option C is incorrect because 'warm' describes the opposite of frozen. Option D is incorrect because 'soft' does not describe frozen water; frozen water is hard, not soft.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences about a butterfly. 'The butterfly's wings were very thin. They were as light as a feather and could tear easily. The baby held the leaf carefully so she would not hurt the delicate insect.' Which details from the text help you understand what the word 'delicate' means? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the fact that the wings could tear easily shows they are fragile and easily damaged, which is what delicate means. Option B is correct because holding the leaf carefully shows that gentle handling is needed for something delicate. Option C is correct because wings that are very thin are easily broken, helping the reader understand delicate means fragile. Option D is incorrect because being as light as a feather describes the weight of the wings, not how easily they can be broken or damaged.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentence: "The elephant was gigantic. It was bigger than a truck and very tall." Which words help you understand what the word "gigantic" means? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option B is correct because comparing the elephant to something 'bigger than a truck' provides a specific example of its large size, which helps define the word 'gigantic'. Option C is correct because 'very tall' describes another aspect of the elephant's large size, helping to clarify that 'gigantic' means extremely big.
subject: ELA

question: Leo is reading a book about dinosaurs. He wants to find the page that tells about T-Rex. He also sees the word "extinct" and wants to know what it means. Which text features can help Leo find this information? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the Table of Contents lists the chapters or topics in the book along with their page numbers, which helps Leo quickly find the page about T-Rex. Option B is correct because a Glossary is a special list at the back of the book that gives the meanings of important words, so Leo can look up "extinct" to learn what it means. Option C is incorrect because the Title only tells the name of the book and does not help locate specific pages or word meanings inside. Option D is incorrect because the Author's name only tells who wrote the book and does not help a reader find specific facts or information within the text.
subject: ELA

question:
Look at the text features from a book about animals.

**Table of Contents**
Chapter 1: Animal Babies ... Page 2
Chapter 2: Animals That Disappeared ... Page 8
Chapter 3: Saving Animals ... Page 12

**Glossary**
Pup: a baby dog or wolf
Extinct: no longer living anywhere on Earth
Habitat: the home of a plant or animal

You want to learn why dinosaurs are not alive today. Which text features will help you find this information? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the chapter titled "Animals That Disappeared" would contain information about animals like dinosaurs that are no longer living. Option B is correct because the glossary defines "Extinct" as "no longer living anywhere on Earth," which matches the description of dinosaurs. Option C is incorrect because "Animal Babies" is about young animals, not animals that have disappeared. Option D is incorrect because "Habitat" refers to where animals live, not whether they are still alive.
subject: ELA

question: Jade is reading a book about birds. She wants to find the page where the chapter 'Eagles' begins. She also wants to know what the word 'feather' means. Which parts of the book should Jade use to find this information? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a table of contents lists the names of chapters and the page numbers where they begin, which helps Jade find the 'Eagles' chapter. Option B is correct because a glossary lists important words from the book and explains what they mean, which helps Jade find the definition of the word 'feather.' Option C is incorrect because a title page only shows the book's title, author, and publisher, not chapter page numbers or word definitions. Option D is incorrect because the cover picture is just an illustration on the front of the book and does not contain information about page numbers or word meanings.
subject: ELA

question: Read these words: 'The cat sleeps.'. It shows a white cat sleeping on a blue bed. Which details are ONLY in the picture and NOT in the words? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.B: The cat is on a bed.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the picture shows the cat is white, but the words do not say anything about color. Option B is correct because the picture shows the cat is on a bed, but the words do not mention a bed. Option C is incorrect because the words say 'The cat sleeps,' so this information is in the text. Option D is incorrect because neither the words nor the picture description mentions the cat being big.
subject: ELA

question: Read the words: 'Bees make honey. They fly from flower to flower.'. Which details can you learn ONLY from the PICTURE? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.A: The bees are yellow and black.
answer_options.D: The hive is in an oak tree.

answer_explanation: A is correct because the colors of the bees (yellow and black) are shown in the picture but not mentioned in the words. D is correct because the location of the hive (in an oak tree) is shown in the picture but not mentioned in the words. B is incorrect because the words state that bees fly ('They fly from flower to flower'), so this detail is found in both the words and the picture, not only the picture. C is incorrect because the words explicitly state that bees make honey, meaning this detail comes from the words, not the picture.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text and. The words say: 'The rabbit hops in the garden.'. Which details can you learn from the picture but NOT from the words? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.D: The rabbit is in the garden.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the picture shows the rabbit is white, but the text does not mention the color. Option C is correct because the picture shows the rabbit has long ears, but the text does not describe the ears. Option B is incorrect because the text explicitly states that the rabbit hops. Option D is incorrect because the text explicitly states the rabbit is in the garden.
subject: ELA

question: Read the words and. Text: It is raining. The puppy plays outside. He splashes in the puddles. Which details tell about the puppy playing in the rain? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.B: The puppy splashes in the puddles.
answer_options.C: The puppy is sleeping on a rug.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the illustration shows the puppy with wet fur, which is a visual detail that helps describe the key idea that the puppy is playing in the rain. Option B is correct because the text explicitly states that the puppy 'splashes in the puddles,' which is a text detail describing the key idea. Option C is incorrect because neither the text nor the illustration shows the puppy sleeping; the text says the puppy plays outside. Option D is incorrect because the text states 'It is raining,' which means the sun is not shining brightly in the illustration.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text and study the illustration description. Select ALL statements that use details from the text AND the illustration to describe the key idea about how frogs grow.

Text: Frogs begin life as tiny eggs in the water. The eggs hatch into tadpoles that swim and have tails. Later, tadpoles grow legs and change into frogs that can hop on land.

Illustration: The picture shows a green frog with long back legs jumping near the edge of a pond. Small black eggs are floating in the water nearby.

answer_options.A: Frogs start as eggs in the water and later grow legs to hop on land.
answer_options.B: The eggs are black and float in the water.
answer_options.C: Tadpoles have tails and swim underwater.
answer_options.D: The frog uses its long legs to jump near the pond.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it combines the text detail that frogs begin as eggs in the water with the illustration showing a frog with legs hopping, describing the key idea of how frogs change as they grow. Option D is correct because it uses the illustration detail of long legs jumping near the pond and connects it to the text explaining that frogs grow legs to hop on land, describing the key idea of the frog's life cycle. Option B is incorrect because it only describes the appearance and location of the eggs shown in the illustration without connecting to the key idea of change or growth. Option C is incorrect because it only repeats text details about tadpoles without using any information from the illustration.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences and. Which details tell what the frog looks like and where it sits? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.B: The frog sits on a big round leaf.
answer_options.D: The frog is flying in the sky.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text says 'green frog' and the picture shows a green frog. Option B is correct because the text says the frog sits on a 'big round leaf' and the picture shows the frog sitting on a round lily pad. Option C is correct because the text says the frog has 'long legs' and the picture shows the frog's long legs. Option D is incorrect because the text says the frog 'sits very still' and the picture shows it sitting on a leaf, not flying.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text. Select ALL the sentences that give reasons why we should eat fruit every day.

We should eat fruit every day. Fruit tastes sweet. Fruit helps keep you healthy. Fruit grows on trees. Fruit can be red.
answer_explanation: The author gives two reasons why we should eat fruit every day: because it tastes sweet (Option A) and because it helps keep you healthy (Option B). Option C tells where fruit grows, which is a fact about fruit but not a reason to eat it given by the author. Option D describes what color fruit can be, which is also a description and not a reason given for eating fruit.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences: "You should eat vegetables every day. Vegetables give you energy to play. They also help your body grow strong." The author wants to convince you to eat vegetables. Select ALL the sentences that give reasons why you should eat vegetables.

answer_options.A: Vegetables give you energy to play.
answer_options.B: They also help your body grow strong.
answer_options.C: You should eat vegetables every day.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the author uses this sentence to explain one reason why eating vegetables is good—giving energy to play. Option B is correct because the author uses this sentence to give another reason—helping your body grow strong. Option C is incorrect because this is the author's main point or opinion, not a reason that supports it. Option D is incorrect because the author does not mention anything about dip or how vegetables taste in the text.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. "You should eat vegetables every day. Vegetables give your body vitamins to help you grow strong. They also have fiber that keeps your tummy healthy." Which reasons does the author give for eating vegetables? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Vegetables give your body vitamins to help you grow strong.
answer_options.B: They have fiber that keeps your tummy healthy.
answer_options.D: Vegetables are fun to pick at the farm.

answer_explanation: The author gives two reasons in the text to support the point that you should eat vegetables every day. Option A is correct because the text states, "Vegetables give your body vitamins to help you grow strong." Option B is correct because the text states, "They also have fiber that keeps your tummy healthy."
subject: ELA

question:
Read these two texts.

Text 1: Frogs can jump far. They have long legs.

Text 2: Frogs breathe air. They live near water.

Select ALL the sentences that correctly tell what is the same or different about the texts.

answer_options.B: Text 1 tells about frog legs, but Text 2 does not.
answer_options.C: Both texts tell that frogs breathe air.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Text 1 and Text 2 are both about frogs, which shows a similarity. Option B is correct because Text 1 mentions that frogs have long legs while Text 2 does not mention legs at all, which shows a difference. Option C is incorrect because only Text 2 mentions that frogs breathe air; Text 1 does not mention breathing. Option D is incorrect because neither text mentions cats; both texts are about frogs.
subject: ELA

question:
Text 1: Rabbits have soft fur and long ears. They eat carrots and green grass. Rabbits can run very fast to hide. They live in burrows under the ground.

Text 2: Rabbits have soft fur and long ears. They eat carrots and lettuce leaves. Rabbits can run very fast to hide. They hop around in gardens.

Which facts are found in BOTH texts? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Rabbits have soft fur and long ears.
answer_options.C: They live in burrows under the ground.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Text 1 and Text 2 state that rabbits have soft fur and long ears. Option B is correct because both texts mention that rabbits eat carrots (Text 1 says 'carrots and green grass' and Text 2 says 'carrots and lettuce leaves'). Option C is incorrect because only Text 1 mentions that rabbits live in burrows; Text 2 does not mention this. Option D is incorrect because only Text 2 mentions that rabbits hop around in gardens; Text 1 does not mention this.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two texts about dogs.

Text 1: Dogs have four legs. Dogs can run fast. Dogs like to play fetch.
Text 2: Dogs have four legs. Dogs can bark loud. Dogs like to play fetch.

Which facts are in BOTH Text 1 and Text 2? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Text 1 states that dogs have four legs and Text 2 also states that dogs have four legs. Option C is correct because Text 1 states that dogs like to play fetch and Text 2 also states that dogs like to play fetch. Option B is incorrect because only Text 1 mentions that dogs can run fast; Text 2 does not mention this fact. Option D is incorrect because only Text 2 mentions that dogs can bark loud; Text 1 does not mention this fact.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. Tom sees a blue truck. The truck goes up the hill. Tom waves at the driver. Which things happen in the story? Select all that apply.

answer_options.B: The truck goes up the hill

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that 'Tom sees a blue truck.' Option B is correct because the story states that 'The truck goes up the hill.' Option D is correct because the story states that 'Tom waves at the driver.' Option C is incorrect because the story does not say that Tom eats a sandwich.
subject: ELA

question: Read the passage: Kim wanted to make lemonade. First, she washed four lemons. Then she squeezed the juice into a pitcher. She added cold water and sugar. Last, she stirred it all up and took a sip. Which details tell steps Kim took to MAKE the lemonade? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: She squeezed the juice into a pitcher.
answer_options.C: She added cold water and sugar.

answer_explanation: Options A, B, and C are correct because washing the lemons, squeezing the juice, and adding water and sugar are all steps in the process of making lemonade. Option D is incorrect because taking a sip is something Kim did after the lemonade was already made; it is an action of drinking, not a step in making the drink.
subject: ELA

question: Tom lost his red hat. He looked under the table. Then he looked behind the door. He found his hat on his bed. Which sentences tell key details from the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: Tom found his hat under the table.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that Tom lost his red hat. Option B is correct because the story says Tom looked under the table. Option C is incorrect because Tom looked under the table but found his hat on his bed, not under the table. Option D is incorrect because the story does not mention Tom looking in the garden.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to find sentences that are good for first graders to read with help. Which sentences use short, easy words that a Grade 1 student could read? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The sun is hot. I eat ice cream.
answer_options.B: Solar radiation causes thermal energy transfer to frozen desserts.
answer_options.C: Rain, rain, go away. Come again another day.
answer_options.D: Meteorological precipitation indicates atmospheric moisture condensation.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses simple words and short sentences that are appropriate for Grade 1. Option C is correct because it is a short poem with easy words and a familiar rhyme pattern suitable for first graders. Option B is incorrect because it uses difficult scientific words like 'radiation' and 'thermal' that are too complex for Grade 1. Option D is incorrect because it uses big words like 'meteorological' and 'condensation' that first graders cannot read yet.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story:

Max the dog lost his red ball. He looked under the bed. He looked behind the sofa. He found it in the garden.

Which sentences show that Max solves his problem? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: Max the dog lost his red ball.
answer_options.D: He found it in the garden.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because looking under the bed is an action Max takes to try to find his ball and solve the problem of losing it. Option B is correct because looking behind the sofa is another action Max takes to search for his ball. Option D is correct because finding the ball in the garden shows Max finally solved his problem. Option C is incorrect because it only tells what the problem is (losing the ball), not how Max solves it.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to pick books that are good for Grade 1 students to read with help. Which books are just right for Grade 1? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A story with short sentences and pictures about a puppy playing in the park.
answer_options.B: A long book with hard words about World War II battles.
answer_options.C: A poem with rhyming words about the colors of fall leaves.
answer_options.D: A science book with big words like "photosynthesis" and "chlorophyll."

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because stories with short sentences, pictures, and familiar topics like puppies match the appropriate complexity for Grade 1 readers. Option C is correct because poems with simple rhyming words and concrete, familiar concepts like fall colors are appropriate poetry for Grade 1. Option B is incorrect because long books with difficult vocabulary and complex historical topics are too hard for Grade 1 students. Option D is incorrect because science books with complex multisyllabic vocabulary like "photosynthesis" exceed the appropriate complexity for Grade 1.
subject: ELA

question: Max saw a bird with a hurt wing. He and his mom took the bird to a vet. The vet helped the bird get better. Later, the bird flew away. Which choices tell key details from the story or show the lesson? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Max saw a bird with a hurt wing.
answer_options.B: Max and his mom took the bird to a vet.
answer_options.C: It is good to help animals that are hurt.
answer_options.D: The vet put the bird in a zoo.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly identifies a key detail from the beginning of the story when Max finds the injured bird. Option B correctly identifies a key detail about the action Max and his mom took to help the bird. Option C correctly states the central message or lesson of the story about helping animals in need.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. Tom saw a bird with a hurt wing. He picked up the bird gently. He asked his mom to help. They put the bird in a box. Tom gave the bird water. After two days, the bird could fly. Tom opened the box and let the bird go. Tom smiled. Which choices tell key details from the story OR show the lesson? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Tom saw a bird with a hurt wing.
answer_options.B: Tom kept the bird as a pet forever.
answer_options.C: Tom and his mom put the bird in a box.
answer_options.D: It is kind to help hurt animals.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it tells a key detail about how Tom found the hurt bird in the story. Option C is correct because it tells a key detail about how Tom and his mom cared for the bird by putting it in a box. Option D is correct because it states the central lesson of the story, which is that helping hurt animals is a kind thing to do. Option B is incorrect because it contradicts the story; Tom did not keep the bird as a pet forever, but instead let it go when it could fly again.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story. Then select ALL sentences that tell a key detail from the story or show the lesson.

Maya is drawing a picture. Her red crayon breaks. Her friend Leo gives her a blue crayon. Maya says thank you and finishes her drawing. They both smile.

answer_options.A: Maya's crayon breaks while she is drawing.
answer_options.B: Leo gives Maya a blue crayon to help her.
answer_options.C: Being kind makes Maya and Leo feel happy.
answer_options.D: Maya draws a picture of a big dinosaur.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it tells a key detail from the beginning of the story: Maya’s crayon breaks while she is drawing. Option B is correct because it tells the key detail about how Leo helps Maya by sharing his crayon. Option C is correct because it states the central message or lesson: helping a friend and being kind makes everyone feel happy. Option D is incorrect because the story does not say what Maya is drawing; it only says she finishes her drawing.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. Ben is a happy puppy. He has soft brown fur. Ben lives in a small house by the lake. One day, Ben jumps into the water to get his stick. Which sentences use key details from the story to describe a character, the setting, or an event? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: Ben lives in a small house by the lake.
answer_options.C: Ben jumps into the water to get his stick.
answer_options.D: Ben eats a bone for dinner.

answer_explanation: Option A uses the key detail 'happy' to describe the character Ben. Option B uses the key details 'small house' and 'by the lake' to describe the setting. Option C uses the key details 'jumps into the water' and 'stick' to describe a major event in the story.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Late one afternoon, Sam and his puppy Bolt went to the sandy beach. Bolt ran fast toward the crashing waves while Sam built a tall castle with wet sand. When the sun began to set, they walked home together.

Which sentences use key details to describe the characters, setting, or events in the story? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The story takes place at a sandy beach late in the day.
answer_options.B: Bolt runs fast toward the crashing waves.
answer_options.C: Sam builds a short tower using dry sand.
answer_options.D: Sam and Bolt walk home together.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses the key details 'sandy beach' and 'late one afternoon' from the text to describe the setting. Option B is correct because it describes the character Bolt's action using the key detail that he 'ran fast toward the crashing waves.' Option D is correct because it describes the major event of walking home using key details from the end of the story. Option C is incorrect because it contradicts the story; the text states Sam built a 'tall castle with wet sand,' not a short tower with dry sand.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Max is a big brown dog. He runs fast in the green yard. Max catches a yellow stick.

Which sentences use key details to describe a character, setting, or event from the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Max is a big brown dog.
answer_options.B: Max runs in the green yard.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly uses the key details 'big' and 'brown' from the story to describe the character Max. Option B correctly uses the key detail 'green yard' from the story to describe the setting where Max runs.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these lines from a poem:

The soft kitten purrs.
I feel happy today.

Which words tell about a feeling or appeal to the senses? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'soft' (Option A) appeals to the sense of touch by describing how the kitten feels. The word 'purrs' (Option B) appeals to the sense of hearing by describing a sound the kitten makes. The word 'happy' (Option C) describes a feeling. The word 'kitten' (Option D) is incorrect because it simply names an animal and does not describe a feeling or appeal to a sense.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. "Maya was angry when the hot soup spilled on her lap. She started to cry, but then she felt the cool breeze from the open window." Which words tell how Maya feels or appeal to the senses? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'angry' is a word that tells how Maya feels. Option B is correct because 'hot' appeals to the sense of touch, describing how the soup feels. Option D is correct because 'cool' appeals to the sense of touch, describing how the breeze feels. Option C is incorrect because 'cry' describes an action Maya does, not a feeling word or a sensory detail.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. The warm cookies smelled sweet. I felt cozy in my soft bed. Which words from the sentences suggest feelings or appeal to the senses? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'warm' appeals to the sense of touch by describing how the cookies feel. The word 'sweet' appeals to the sense of smell or taste. The word 'cozy' suggests a comfortable feeling or emotion. The word 'bed' is incorrect because it names an object and does not describe a feeling or appeal to the senses.
subject: ELA

question: Which of these are found in books that give information? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Options A, B, and D are correct because books that give information contain photographs of real things, include a table of contents to help readers find topics, and teach true facts. Option C is incorrect because made-up characters appear in books that tell stories, not in books that give information.
subject: ELA

question: Maya is comparing two books. One is a story book called 'The Lion and the Mouse' that tells about animal friends. The other is an informational book called 'Lions: True Facts' that teaches about real lions. Which statements show major differences between these two types of books? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The story book has characters that talk to each other, but the informational book has photographs with labels.
answer_options.B: The informational book teaches real facts, while the story book tells made-up events.
answer_options.C: Both books have the same number of pages.
answer_options.D: The story book has a beginning, middle, and end that solve a problem, while the informational book has sections with headings.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly identifies that story books use characters and dialogue to tell a tale, whereas informational books use visual features like labeled photographs to present facts. Option B correctly distinguishes the content types: informational books provide factual information about real things, while story books present fictional narratives about made-up events. Option D correctly contrasts the organizational structures: story books follow a narrative arc with a problem and solution, while informational books use headings to categorize factual information.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to find books that give information. Which features help you know a book teaches facts about real things? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The book teaches real facts about sharks.
answer_options.B: The book tells a story about a dragon who breathes fire.
answer_options.C: The book has photographs of real animals.
answer_options.D: The book has headings that tell what each part is about.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because books that give information teach real facts about the world. Option C is correct because photographs of real animals are used in informational books to show true things. Option D is correct because headings help organize facts in books that give information. Option B is incorrect because stories about dragons that breathe fire are made up and belong in story books, not information books.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these sentences from a story.

"I like to jump in puddles. I wear my yellow boots."

Who is telling this part of the story? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the narrator describes wearing yellow boots, so the person wearing them is the one telling the story. Option B is correct because the story uses the word 'I,' which shows that a character in the story is the narrator. Option C is incorrect because puddles are objects and cannot tell a story. Option D is incorrect because boots are objects and cannot tell a story.
subject: ELA

question:
Read this short story.

The frog sat on a log.
"I see a tasty fly!" said the frog.
The fly buzzed away.

Which of the following correctly describe who is telling each part of the story? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The narrator tells the first sentence.
answer_options.B: The frog tells the second sentence.
answer_options.C: The narrator tells the second sentence.
answer_options.D: The frog tells the third sentence.

answer_explanation: The first sentence uses the words "The frog" to describe the character from outside the story, which shows the narrator is telling this part. The second sentence uses the word "I" inside quotation marks along with the tag "said the frog," which shows the frog is speaking for himself and telling this part of the story.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences below. Select ALL sentences that are told by a character in the story.

answer_options.A: I like to play with my ball.
answer_options.B: The boy runs to the park.
answer_options.C: My dog is soft and warm.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'I' indicates that a character in the story is speaking. Option C is correct because the word 'My' shows that a character is describing their own dog, meaning the character is telling this part of the story. Option B is incorrect because it uses 'The boy,' which signals that an outside narrator is describing the boy's actions. Option D is incorrect because it uses 'She,' which signals that an outside narrator is describing what another character is doing.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. 'The park is sunny. The green grass is soft. Kim plays on the slide. She has a yellow kite. Birds fly in the sky.' Which phrases describe the setting? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'the sunny park' names the place where the story happens. Option B is correct because 'the green grass' describes the ground at the park, which is part of the setting. Option D is correct because 'the tall slide' describes playground equipment at the park, which is also part of the setting.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. 'Maya walked to school on a windy morning. Leaves blew across the path.' The illustration shows a girl walking on a sidewalk next to a tall brick building with many windows. Yellow and orange leaves are swirling in the air around her. Which phrases describe the SETTING in this story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The sidewalk next to the school
answer_options.B: The tall brick building with many windows
answer_options.C: The girl in the red coat
answer_options.D: The swirling yellow and orange leaves

answer_explanation: The setting is where and when the story takes place. Option A is correct because the sidewalk and path describe where Maya is walking. Option B is correct because the tall brick building with windows describes the location of the school area. Option D is correct because the swirling leaves describe the windy weather and environment around her. Option C is incorrect because it describes what the character (the girl) is wearing, not the place or time of the story.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story and look at the illustration.

Story: Maya is visiting her grandmother's farm. She walks up the dirt path to the big red barn to feed the chickens.

Which phrases describe the setting shown in the illustration? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the red barn with chickens is part of the farm setting shown in the illustration. Option B is correct because the dirt path is a feature of the setting where Maya walks. Option C is incorrect because the blue coat describes what the character is wearing, not the setting itself. Option D is incorrect because the illustration does not show tall corn growing in the scene.
subject: ELA

question: Ben builds a sandcastle at the beach. Ben eats a sandwich for lunch. Mia builds a snowman in the yard. Mia eats a sandwich for lunch. Which things are the same for Ben and Mia? Select all that apply.

answer_options.B: They both eat sandwiches for lunch.
answer_options.C: They both play at the beach.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Ben and Mia build things (Ben builds a sandcastle and Mia builds a snowman). Option B is correct because the text states both Ben and Mia eat a sandwich for lunch. Option C is incorrect because only Ben plays at the beach; Mia plays in the yard. Option D is incorrect because only Ben builds a sandcastle; Mia builds a snowman.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Tom and Kim go on trips. Tom sails a boat on the lake. He sees fish jump. Kim hikes up a mountain. She sees birds fly. Both Tom and Kim feel brave. Both go on outdoor adventures.

Which sentences tell how Tom and Kim are the SAME? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: They both go on outdoor adventures.
answer_options.D: Tom sails a boat and Kim hikes up a mountain.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story says both Tom and Kim feel brave. Option B is correct because Tom sees fish and Kim sees birds, which are both animals. Option C is correct because the story states both go on outdoor adventures (trips). Option D is incorrect because it describes how the characters are different, not the same: Tom sails while Kim hikes.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two stories.

Story 1: Maya climbs a tree. She sees a nest. She is careful.
Story 2: Carlos climbs a tree. He sees a squirrel. He is careful.

Which sentences describe how Maya and Carlos are the same or different? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.D: They see different things in the tree.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Maya and Carlos climb a tree in their stories. Option C is correct because both characters are described as being careful. Option D is correct because Maya sees a nest while Carlos sees a squirrel, showing they see different things. Option B is incorrect because only Maya sees a nest; Carlos sees a squirrel instead.
subject: ELA

question: The class is having a discussion about their favorite animals. Which students are following the rules for a good discussion? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maria waits for her turn before speaking.
answer_options.B: Tom looks at the speaker and listens carefully.
answer_options.C: Sara talks about her lunch while others discuss animals.
answer_options.D: Jake interrupts his friend to tell his story.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Maria waits for her turn, which follows the rule to speak one at a time. Option B is correct because Tom looks at the speaker and listens carefully, which follows the rule to listen to others with care. Option C is incorrect because Sara talks about lunch when the topic is animals, so she is not staying on the topic under discussion. Option D is incorrect because Jake interrupts his friend, which breaks the rule of speaking one at a time.
subject: ELA

question: During sharing time, four friends are discussing a story they just read. The class rules say to listen with care, speak one at a time, and stay on the topic. Which friends are following these rules? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya raises her hand and waits quietly until the teacher calls her name to speak.
answer_options.B: Leo looks at the speaker and sits still to show he is paying attention.
answer_options.C: Zoe whispers to her neighbor about what she will eat for lunch.
answer_options.D: Jake blurts out his idea while another student is still talking.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because raising a hand and waiting to be called on follows the rule of speaking one at a time. Option B is correct because looking at the speaker and sitting still demonstrates listening with care.
subject: ELA

question: During circle time, the class is discussing their favorite animals. Which students are following the rules for discussion? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Jade raises her hand and waits for the teacher to call on her before she speaks.
answer_options.B: Marcus looks at the speaker and nods his head to show he is listening.
answer_options.C: Emma whispers to her friend about her cat while another student is talking.
answer_options.D: David shouts out his answer without waiting for his turn.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Jade waits for her turn to speak, following the rule of speaking one at a time. Option B is correct because Marcus shows he is listening with care by looking at the speaker and nodding. Option C is incorrect because Emma is whispering while someone else is talking, which means she is not listening with care. Option D is incorrect because David shouts out without waiting, which breaks the rule of speaking one at a time.
subject: ELA

question: During sharing time, Lee says, "I went to the beach and built a big sandcastle." Which sentences show ways to build on Lee's talk and keep the conversation going? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Did you use a bucket to make it?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking about the bucket shows interest in how Lee built the sandcastle. Option B is correct because asking about the shape requests more details about the sandcastle Lee described. Option C is incorrect because it changes the topic to pizza instead of responding to Lee's comment about the beach. Option D is incorrect because it asks about Lee's brother rather than building on the comment about the sandcastle.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the conversation between Ben and Ava.

Ben: "I saw a deer in my backyard this morning."
Ava: "Was it eating leaves?"
Ben: "Yes, it was eating from the bushes."
Ava: "I like to ride my bike fast."
Ben: "The deer had big antlers."
Ava: "Were the antlers pointy?"

Which sentences show Ava building on Ben’s talk to keep the conversation going? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: I like to ride my bike fast.
answer_options.D: Do you want to play a game?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Ava asks about what the deer was doing, which builds on Ben’s comment about seeing the deer. Option C is correct because Ava asks about the antlers right after Ben mentions them, showing she is responding to his specific comment. Option B is incorrect because talking about riding a bike changes the topic away from the deer Ben is describing. Option D is incorrect because asking to play a game does not respond to or build on Ben’s comment about the deer’s antlers.
subject: ELA

question: During sharing time, Leo says, "My grandma gave me a new puppy yesterday." Which responses build on what Leo said to keep the conversation going? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I would like to meet your puppy.
answer_options.B: I have a pet at home too.

answer_explanation: Option A builds on Leo's comment by showing interest in meeting his new puppy. Option B builds on Leo's comment by sharing a related experience about having a pet.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher reads a story about Max losing his hat. Which questions can you ask to clear up confusion about the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Where did Max lose his hat?
answer_options.B: Did Max find his hat at the end?

answer_explanation: Standard SL.1.1.C is about asking questions to clear up confusion about a topic or text under discussion. Option A is correct because asking where Max lost his hat clarifies the location of the event in the story, which helps clear up confusion about what happened. Option B is correct because asking whether Max found his hat clarifies how the story ends, addressing potential confusion about the resolution. Option C is incorrect because it is a statement describing a fact, not a question that seeks to clarify confusion. Option D is incorrect because it is a question about the listener’s drawing, which is unrelated to the story about Max and does not help clear up confusion about the text.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher is reading a story about a boy named Max. She says, 'Max was playing at the park. He put his favorite hat down on a bench. When he turned around, the hat was gone. He looked everywhere but could not find it.' Which questions would help clear up confusion about the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Which park was Max playing at?
answer_options.C: Did Max put his hat on a bench?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story mentions Max was at 'the park' but does not say which park, so asking this would clear up that confusion. Option B is correct because the story mentions a 'favorite hat' but does not describe what it looks like, so asking about the color would clear up that confusion. Option C is incorrect because the story already stated that Max put his hat on a bench, so this question would not clear up any confusion. Option D is incorrect because the story already stated that Max could not find his hat, so this question would not clear up any confusion.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher read a story about Emma who lost her red mitten at school. The story says Emma looked for the mitten in two places, but you are confused about where she searched. Which questions help clear up your confusion about the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Where did Emma look for her mitten?
answer_options.B: Did Emma look in her classroom or the playground?
answer_options.C: Who found the mitten for Emma?
answer_options.D: Do you have a red mitten too?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking 'Where did Emma look for her mitten?' directly clears up confusion about the locations she searched. Option B is correct because it asks a specific question about which places she searched, clarifying the exact locations. Option C is incorrect because it asks about who found the mitten, which does not address the confusion about where Emma searched. Option D is incorrect because it asks for personal information rather than clarifying details from the story.
subject: ELA

question: The teacher read a story aloud to the class: 'Maria visited the farm. She saw three brown horses. She fed some baby goats. She picked red apples.' What did the teacher say Maria did at the farm? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the teacher said Maria saw three brown horses. Option B is correct because the teacher said Maria fed some baby goats. Option C is correct because the teacher said Maria picked red apples. Option D is incorrect because the teacher did not say Maria rode a tractor; riding a tractor is a possible farm activity but was not mentioned in the story read aloud.
subject: ELA

question: Listen to this story: 'Maya's grandmother visited yesterday. She wore a purple coat and carried a striped umbrella. They baked twelve chocolate cookies together. Then they looked at photos of a snowy mountain.' Which things happened in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The grandmother wore a purple coat
answer_options.C: They looked at photos of a snowy mountain
answer_options.D: The grandmother carried a spotted umbrella

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that the grandmother 'wore a purple coat.' Option B is correct because the story says 'They baked twelve chocolate cookies together.' Option C is correct because the story mentions they 'looked at photos of a snowy mountain.' Option D is incorrect because the story specifies the grandmother carried a 'striped umbrella,' not a spotted one; this is a near-miss distractor that changes a key detail.
subject: ELA

question: Listen to what the teacher tells the class: 'Yesterday I visited the aquarium. I watched the penguins swim underwater for ten minutes. They were black and white and moved very fast through the clear water.' Which details did the teacher share about her visit? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The teacher watched penguins swim underwater.
answer_options.C: The penguins were black and white.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the teacher explicitly states that she watched the penguins swim underwater. Option C is correct because the teacher describes the penguins as black and white. Option B is incorrect because the teacher visited the aquarium, not the zoo. Option D is incorrect because the teacher states the penguins moved very fast, not slowly.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher says, 'Tomorrow we will plant seeds in the garden.' Which questions could you ask to learn more about what the teacher said or to understand something that is not clear? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What kind of seeds will we plant?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking what kind of seeds gathers additional information about the seeds the teacher mentioned. Option B is correct because asking where the garden is clarifies the location the teacher mentioned. Option C is not correct because liking ice cream is unrelated to planting seeds. Option D is not correct because shirt color does not help clarify or add information about the teacher's statement.
subject: ELA

question: Tom says, 'I have a big day tomorrow. I hope my stuff fits in my bag.' Select ALL the questions that would help you understand what Tom said better.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks Tom to clarify what he means by 'the big day.' Option B is correct because it asks Tom to clarify what 'stuff' he is putting in his bag. Option D is correct because it asks for additional information about when Tom is going.
subject: ELA

question: A classmate says, "I found a strange bug in my backyard this morning." Which questions would help you learn more about what your classmate said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: Where in the backyard did you find it?
answer_options.C: What time do we eat lunch?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking about the color gathers additional information about the bug the classmate found. Option B is correct because asking where in the backyard clarifies the specific location of the discovery. Option C is incorrect because asking about lunch time does not relate to the classmate's statement about the bug. Option D is incorrect because asking about a best friend does not clarify or add information about the bug found in the backyard.
subject: ELA

question: Lily is telling her class about her new kitten. Which sentences describe the kitten with details or express how Lily feels? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: My kitten has soft, white fur.
answer_options.B: She likes to chase a ball of yarn.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'soft, white fur' provides specific descriptive details about the kitten's appearance. Option B is correct because 'chase a ball of yarn' provides specific details about the kitten's behavior. Option C is correct because 'I love her so much' clearly expresses Lily's feelings about the kitten. Option D is incorrect because it only identifies what the animal is without providing any specific descriptive details about its features, actions, or feelings.
subject: ELA
